The conclusion identifies Fortinet as the leading candidate despite debt concerns due to strong financials, while noting Palo Alto Networks' appealing valuation and CrowdStrike's recent setbacks.

title: Visa and Mastercard customers can get cash from $197.5m surcharge settlement - qualifying depends on your ATM use
summary: Visa and Mastercard have agreed to a $197.5 million settlement for allegedly imposing unlawful ATM surcharges and excessive interchange swipe fees. Customers who paid ATM surcharges between October 1, 2007, and July 26, 2024, through member banks like JPMorgan Chase, Wells Fargo, and Bank of America, are eligible to file a claim. The deadline to submit a claim is January 22, 2025, and payments will vary based on the number of valid claims received.

title: FDL: Makes Building A Dividend Portfolio Easy, But Has Weaknesses
summary: The First Trust Morningstar Dividend Leaders Index Fund (FDL) offers an accessible way to invest in dividend-paying companies with a 4% yield and 0.45% expense ratio, providing diversification and stability. While it simplifies portfolio building and eliminates single stock risk with an 8.2% dividend CAGR over a decade, its lack of tech exposure and superior dividend growth makes it better suited as an ancillary part of a dividend portfolio. The ETF's diverse holdings, including Chevron, Verizon, and AbbVie, offer balanced dividend growth but may have lower starting yields compared to individual stocks.

title: Citi, JP Morgan, others complete tokenization settlement trials
summary: Ten institutions, including Citi and J.P. Morgan, have completed simulated trials of the Regulated Settlement Network (RSN). These trials explored the use of a shared DLT network for settling tokenized transactions, building on previous Regulated Liability Network trials. The latest tests included tokenized Treasuries and investment-grade bonds, enabling delivery versus payment for wholesale transactions and exploring interoperability with external DLT networks.

title: OwlTing Partners With MoneyGram; Nuvei Enables Blockchain Payment in Latin America; Nacha’s Crypto Report
summary: OwlTing Group is partnering with MoneyGram to integrate its OwlPay Wallet Pro service with MoneyGram Access, allowing cash-in and cash-out transactions for USD Coin across 30 countries and aiding financial inclusion in underbanked regions. Separately, Nuvei Corp. launched a blockchain-based payment solution for Latin American merchants using stablecoins via Visa-branded cards, and Nacha published a report on cryptocurrency to help financial institutions understand digital payments.

title: Global Payments spots 6 trends for next year
summary: Global Payments predicts six key trends for 2025, including enhanced fraud-fighting tools, embedded payments, and comprehensive point-of-sale systems. Their report, based on a survey of 600 companies, highlights increased investment in embedded payments and digital wallets by small and mid-sized businesses, along with the growing use of AI in biometrics and tap-to-pay transactions. The article also discusses the adoption of unified commerce platforms and payment orchestration across various sectors.

title: ROSEN, THE FIRST FILING FIRM, Encourages Visa Inc.
summary: The Rosen Law Firm is reminding Visa Inc. investors who purchased securities between November 16, 2023, and September 23, 2024, of an important January 21, 2025 lead plaintiff deadline for a securities class action. The lawsuit alleges that Visa made false or misleading statements and failed to disclose non-compliance with federal antitrust laws during the Class Period. Investors may be entitled to compensation and are encouraged to contact the firm for more information.

title: Small Deposits: NJ bank scoops up lenders from failed Republic First Bank
summary: Provident Bank has significantly expanded its local lending team by hiring lenders from the recently failed Republic First Bank. In related financial news, TD Bank Group announced a new chief auditor and Vanguard has added actively managed ETFs to its offerings. This move by Provident Bank will double its local lending capabilities.

title: ROSEN, THE FIRST FILING FIRM, Encourages Visa Inc.
summary: The Rosen Law Firm is encouraging investors who purchased Visa Inc. securities between November 16, 2023, and September 23, 2024, to secure legal counsel before the January 21, 2025, lead plaintiff deadline for a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Visa made materially false or misleading statements by failing to disclose non-compliance with federal antitrust laws and inadequate internal compliance programs. Investors who suffered damages can join the class action to seek compensation.

title: Democrats bash CapOne-Discover merger proposal
summary: A group of Democratic lawmakers, including Sen. Elizabeth Warren and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, is urging federal regulators to closely scrutinize Capital One's proposed $35-billion acquisition of Discover Financial Services. They cite Capital One's history of regulatory infractions, including broken promises on community investment and penalties for consumer product abuses and anti-money laundering failures, as reasons for concern. Despite the opposition, financial services experts suggest the merger is likely to be approved due to potential benefits in credit card network competition.

title: WestBridge Capital Offloads 2% Stake In Freshworks
summary: WestBridge Capital Management sold 2.75 Lakh shares, representing 2% of its stake, in Nasdaq-listed SaaS company Freshworks during the quarter ended September 2024. This transaction reduced WestBridge Capital's holding to just over 4%, valued at approximately $221 million. The news comes as Freshworks narrowed its consolidated net loss by 3.5% in Q3 2024, despite recently announcing layoffs and a $400 million stock buyback program.

title: Brown Advisory Flexible Equity ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:BAFE)
summary: This article provides detailed information about the Brown Advisory Flexible Equity ETF (BAFE), including its current price, key statistics, holdings, and recent news. BAFE is an actively managed ETF that invests in a concentrated portfolio of US mid- and large-cap equity securities. The fund's top holdings include major tech and financial companies like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, Microsoft, and Meta Platforms.

title: Logitech International S.A. (VTX:LOGN) is largely controlled by institutional shareholders who own 67% of the company
summary: Logitech International S.A. (VTX:LOGN) is primarily controlled by institutional shareholders, who collectively own 67% of the company, indicating the stock price is sensitive to their trading actions. BlackRock, Inc., Acadian Asset Management LLC, and The Vanguard Group, Inc. are among the largest institutional holders. Insiders own less than 1% but have been observed buying shares recently, while the general public holds a 33% stake.

title: Ryvyl Inc (RVYL) Q3 2024 Earnings Call Highlights: Global Expansion and Revenue Challenges
summary: Ryvyl Inc (RVYL) reported significant international revenue growth in Q3 2024, driven by global expansion and strategic partnerships, despite a decrease in total revenue and negative adjusted EBITDA. The company also faced delays in European software integrations, impacting financial performance, and adjusted its 2024 revenue guidance downward. Ryvyl anticipates substantial growth in 2025, particularly in cryptocurrency and demand for conversion services in Latin America.

title: Paying Attention to Investment Factors? You Should Be
summary: This article explores the concept of factor investing, noting that market leadership among factors like value, quality, and momentum is constantly changing. It details various investment factors, their historical performance, and why they might or might not continue to be successful, urging investors to consider them when building portfolios. The author questions whether quality and momentum, despite their recent strong performance, will continue to lead, and discusses the rationale behind sticking with underperforming factors like low volatility and size.

title: Booking Holdings Announces Job Cuts Amid Restructuring
summary: Booking Holdings is implementing job cuts as part of a restructuring effort to enhance operational efficiency and reduce costs, following a 13.6% rise in operating expenses in Q3 2024. This move aligns with a broader industry trend of major companies streamlining operations in response to rising costs, contributing to increased uncertainty in the job market. The company plans to modernize processes and optimize procurement, though specific details on job cuts are yet to be released.

title: Layoffs: Booking.Com Considering Job Cuts As Part Of Reorganisation
summary: Booking.com is planning job cuts as part of a review of its organizational structure. The restructuring is specific to Booking.com and will not affect other Booking Holdings brands. The company aims to improve operating expense efficiency and organizational agility, following a 13.6 percent increase in operating expenses for the third quarter.

title: ANTITRUST NEWS: Class action alleging VISA debit card monopoly filed in New York (Nov 8, 2024)
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed in New York, accusing VISA of operating a debit card monopoly. This legal action targets alleged anti-competitive practices by VISA in the debit card market.

title: Blackhawk Network Launches Digital Cards With Its Visa And Mastercard Branded e-Gift Products
summary: Blackhawk Network has launched new Visa and Mastercard branded e-gift cards that operate as digital-only products, leveraging QR code and tap-to-pay technology. These cards are designed to enhance security by encrypting card numbers and minimize fraud risk, building on consumer preference for digital payment methods. Consumers can scan a QR code at purchase to load funds directly to their mobile wallets for immediate online or in-store use.

title: Fiserv, Capital One, others invest $150M in Melio
summary: Melio, an accounts payable and receivable company for SMBs, has raised $150 million in new strategic funding from investors including Fiserv and Capital One's venture arm. The company, valued at $2 billion, plans to use the capital to expand through B2B partnerships, strengthening existing ties with investors like Fiserv and Shopify. This investment follows previous collaborations between Melio and Fiserv, and Melio and Shopify, integrating payment tools and bill pay services into their respective networks.

title: Is Visa a Debit Monopolist?
summary: The U.S. Justice Department has filed a lawsuit against Visa, alleging anti-competitive practices in the debit market. The DoJ claims Visa maintains dominance through exclusive contracts with card issuers that include incentives and punitive fees, thereby stifling competition and preventing rivals from gaining necessary scale. Visa, however, refutes these claims, stating that the debit market is dynamic and competitive, asserting it will vigorously defend itself against the "meritless" lawsuit.

title: Franco Colapinto, divino tesoro: qué empresas lo ignoraron y ahora corren a esponsorearlo
summary: The article discusses how Argentine companies initially ignored Franco Colapinto's career in F2 but are now eager to sponsor him following his move to F1. It highlights that out of 100 companies contacted for F2 sponsorship, 85 declined, while now there's significant marketing interest. Key figures like Marcos Galperin (Mercado Libre) and Globant, prompted by Bizarrap, have joined as sponsors, showcasing Colapinto's growing global appeal likened to Lionel Messi.

title: Mastercard Earnings: Settling Into a Groove
summary: Mastercard's third-quarter earnings show increasing stability, mirroring Visa's performance but with slightly better margins. The company's long-term growth prospects and strong profitability are becoming clearer, with net revenue up 14% year over year and payment volume increasing by 10%. Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate of $465 per share, considering the stock to be fairly valued.

title: Institutional investors in Alerus Financial Corporation (NASDAQ:ALRS) see US$64m decrease in market cap last week, although long-term gains have benefitted them.
summary: Institutional investors in Alerus Financial Corporation (ALRS) experienced a US$64m decrease in market cap last week, despite benefiting from 22% one-year returns. Institutions hold 49% ownership, making their trading decisions influential on the stock price. The top 20 shareholders collectively own 50% of the company, with Alerus Financial Corp., Asset Management Arm as the largest individual shareholder.

title: Visa class action alleges company monopolizes debit card networks
summary: A new class action lawsuit has been filed against Visa Inc., alleging the company monopolizes debit card networks and charges artificially inflated prices for its debit products. The plaintiff, Yabla Inc., claims Visa engages in exclusionary conduct to stifle competition and protect its highly profitable debit business. The lawsuit seeks to represent merchants in the U.S. who have accepted Visa-branded debit cards since October 22, 2020, and alleges Sherman Act violations.

title: Visa CEO stands fast against DOJ
summary: Visa CEO Ryan McInerney has strongly denounced the Justice Department's antitrust lawsuit against the company, calling it "meritless" and based on a misunderstanding of the debit card ecosystem. McInerney stated that Visa will vigorously defend itself, asserting that it operates in a competitive debit space. He also addressed the broader regulatory landscape and new payment methods, affirming Visa's commitment to innovation and growth despite legal challenges.

title: Palmetto Grain Brokerage - Previous Tech Stock Strength Lifts the Broader Market
summary: The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrials saw gains, primarily driven by strong tech stock performance and better-than-expected US economic data, including Q3 personal consumption and ADP employment figures. However, the Nasdaq 100 experienced pressure from negative corporate news concerning Super Micro Computer and Advanced Micro Devices. The article also touches on fluctuating interest rates, key upcoming economic reports, and earnings season updates.

title: Visa Earnings: A Stable Fiscal Year Finishes on a Good Note
summary: Visa concluded its fiscal year with a stable fourth quarter, reinforcing its position in a comfortable groove as pandemic, political, and macro effects have dissipated. The company showed consistent growth and strong profitability, evidenced by a 12% year-over-year increase in constant currency net revenue and an 8% rise in payment volume. Cross-border volume normalization and muted client incentive growth further contribute to the positive outlook, with Morningstar maintaining its $272 fair value estimate.

title: Visa announces Q4 and full-year fiscal 2024 results
summary: Visa Inc. (NYSE: V) reported its financial results for Q4 and the full fiscal year ending September 30, 2024, highlighting robust earnings and announcing a quarterly cash dividend of $0.59 per share. The company also announced strategic initiatives like the Visa Tokenized Asset Platform and the Visa Commercial Solutions Hub, alongside recent workforce reductions and varying analyst ratings. InvestingPro insights underscore Visa's strong market capitalization, consistent dividend increases, and high profitability margins.

title: Milei’s revamp offers CEOs a glimmer of optimism about Argentina
summary: Javier Milei's presidency in Argentina, marked by peso devaluation and aggressive austerity, is starting to bring a sense of optimism among business leaders. Despite the economy being in recession, executives are encouraged by his moves to eliminate protectionist measures and red tape, seeing a clearer path to profitability. While new investments have been slow, a growing sentiment suggests this time might be different for Argentina's long-struggling economy.

title: Insight Partners has paid out nearly $8 billion this year—but returns for its giant 2021 fund have mostly come up dry
summary: Insight Partners has returned nearly $8 billion to investors this year, a record high for the firm. However, most of these returns have not come from its $20 billion Fund XII, launched in 2021, which currently shows a negative net IRR. The fund's underperformance is attributed to the high valuations paid for companies during the 2021 tech bubble and the subsequent market "reset" in 2022, though experts suggest it is still too early to definitively judge the fund's long-term success.

title: Rosen Law Firm Encourages Visa Inc. Investors to Inquire About Securities Class Action Investigation - V
summary: The Rosen Law Firm is investigating potential securities claims on behalf of Visa Inc. investors following allegations that Visa issued materially misleading business information. This investigation comes after the U.S. Department of Justice filed a civil antitrust lawsuit against Visa for monopolizing debit markets, causing Visa's stock to fall 5.4% on September 24, 2024. Investors who purchased Visa securities may be entitled to compensation through a class action lawsuit.

title: State of consumer digital payments in 2024
summary: Digital payment adoption continues to rise in the US and Europe, with a notable shift toward consumers initiating their shopping journeys with digital payments, not just using them at checkout. The article highlights the growth of in-app and in-store digital payments, the divergence of digital wallet preferences across regions and channels, and opportunities for ecosystem players to engage consumers earlier and innovate with rewards and account-to-account payments.

title: NY Attorney General launches probe into Capital One’s $35.3bn deal with Discover Financial
summary: New York Attorney General Letitia James has launched an antitrust investigation into Capital One Financial's proposed $35.3bn acquisition of Discover Financial Services. The probe aims to determine if the merger violates state antitrust laws, given the significant credit card loan portfolios of both companies within New York and potential impacts on vulnerable consumers. Capital One has affirmed its pro-competitive stance and confidence in regulatory approval despite the investigation and customer lawsuits.

title: CLO ETFs: Resilient in the Face of Lower Rates?
summary: This article discusses the resiliency of CLO ETFs amid a changing economic climate characterized by lower interest rates. Despite anticipated rate cuts, CLO ETFs continue to offer compelling yields and risk-adjusted returns, appealing to both institutional and retail investors seeking floating-rate assets and diversification. The market for CLO ETFs is rapidly expanding, with new products and increased assets under management highlighting their growing appeal.

title: Argentina to make available additional spectrum for 5G services
summary: The Argentine government announced it will release additional spectrum for 5G services, totaling 150 megahertz, previously held by state company Arsat and regulator Enacom. This move aims to stimulate investment and improve telecommunications in the country. This follows a 2023 tender where Claro, Movistar, and Personal acquired spectrum, with 5G penetration expected to reach 48% by 2030 in Argentina.

title: VanEck establishes $30 million venture fund to support crypto and AI startups
summary: VanEck has launched a new $30 million venture fund, VanEck Ventures, to invest in early-stage companies in fintech, digital assets, and AI sectors. Led by Wyatt Lonergan and Juan Lopez, the fund targets investments between $500,000 and $1 million for 25-30 projects, with a focus on tokenization, internet-native financial marketplaces, and next-generation payment systems, particularly stablecoin innovations. This initiative aligns with VanEck's strategy to expand its involvement in the digital asset space beyond its existing ETF offerings.

title: Visa and Mastercard: Top Payment Stocks to Watch After Rate Cuts
summary: This article discusses the investment prospects of Visa (NYSE: V) and Mastercard (NYSE: MA) after the Federal Reserve's recent rate cut. While the rate cut's impact on variable-rate credit cards may be minimal, the consistently high APR margins are beneficial for payment processing companies. The article examines each company's growth potential, noting Visa's strong earnings outlook despite recent antitrust lawsuits, and Mastercard's strategic acquisition of Recorded Future for enhanced cybersecurity and value-added services.

title: McKesson, Cencora slapped with $266M in damages in Baltimore opioid lawsuit
summary: A Baltimore court has ordered McKesson and Cencora (formerly AmerisourceBergen) to pay $266 million in damages to the city for their role in the opioid crisis. The judgment comes after a 2022 ruling that found the two drug distributors liable for causing a public nuisance. This decision adds to billions of dollars already paid by opioid manufacturers and distributors to settle similar lawsuits across the country.

title: 10 drugmakers that rule the pharmaceutical industry
summary: S&P Global ranked the world's top pharmaceutical companies based on business strength and financial risk. Johnson & Johnson and Roche led the list with excellent business and minimal financial risk profiles. The article details the strengths and weaknesses of 10 major drugmakers, including their revenue, product diversity, and market position.

title: Partnering with Customers and Leveraging Data to Boost Health
summary: Kenvue, spun off from Johnson & Johnson, has focused on leveraging data and strategic partnerships with retailers to optimize its supply chain operations. This approach has led to reduced unloading times, overages, damages, shortages, and improved on-time shipment arrivals. The company utilizes technology, including AI, to enhance order-to-cash processes, automate order intake, improve picking/packing efficiency, and implement a "Perfect Store" solution to prevent out-of-stocks at the retail shelf level.

title: Moderna | History, Innovation, Challenges, & Facts
summary: Moderna, a biotechnology company founded in 2010, specializes in messenger RNA (mRNA) medicines and is best known for developing one of the first COVID-19 vaccines. The company researches treatments for infectious diseases, cancer, and rare genetic disorders, actively exploring new uses for mRNA technology. Moderna's rapid growth has led to significant advancements, but also challenges including patent disputes and scrutiny over executive stock sales.

title: TDF Litigation
summary: Gilead Sciences is facing litigation in California over its TDF-based HIV medications, where plaintiffs argue the company should have developed TAF-based medicines sooner. The California Supreme Court is reviewing an appeals court decision that would impose a new legal obligation on manufacturers to develop improved products, a theory Gilead and many amici curiae argue could stifle innovation. Gilead maintains that it developed TDF and later TAF medications responsibly, with both drug types currently approved and recommended for HIV treatment.

title: Nina Barton
summary: Nina Barton is the Executive Vice President and Group President – Care & Connection at The Clorox Company, a position she assumed in July 2024. In this role, she is responsible for driving the company's Care & Connection businesses, including Brita, Burt’s Bees, Food, Glad, Kingsford and Litter. Barton brings over 25 years of consumer packaged goods experience, previously serving as CEO of Vytalogy Wellness and global chief growth officer at The Kraft Heinz Company.

title: Abdera Therapeutics Expands Management Team and Board of Directors
summary: Abdera Therapeutics announced the appointments of Philippe Bishop, M.D., as chief medical officer and Eric Dobmeier to its board of directors. Dr. Bishop brings extensive oncology drug development experience, which will be crucial as Abdera advances its lead DLL3 program, ABD-147, into clinical trials. Mr. Dobmeier, former CEO of Chinook Therapeutics, adds significant company-building and corporate development expertise, aligning with Abdera's goal of becoming a leading radiopharmaceutical company.

title: Why Takeda no longer has an 'IT department' as part of the drug maker's digital and data transformation
summary: Takeda Pharmaceutical, under Chief Data and Technology Officer Gabriele Ricci, has eliminated the term "IT department" to emphasize technology as a core business function. The company has substantially reorganized its technology division, bringing most technical work in-house by hiring thousands of engineers and data scientists, and investing heavily in digital and AI transformation to adapt to a volatile pharmaceutical environment. Takeda is actively upskilling its workforce in new technologies, including generative AI tools like "myAibou," and is building and buying AI models while ensuring ethical AI practices.

title: AI revolutionising everything from patient care to health communication: Delhi AIIMS director
summary: AIIMS, Delhi, is investing over Rs 300 crore in digital infrastructure to integrate AI, enhancing efficiency, reducing delays, and enabling research across patient care and health communication. Experts highlighted AI's role in simplifying health information and empowering patients but emphasized balancing technology with human empathy for compassionate care. The article also features Humeta, an AI model by Healthpresso, designed to make complex medical concepts accessible and reliable.

title: Merck (NYSE:MRK) Has A Pretty Healthy Balance Sheet
summary: This article analyzes Merck's financial health, specifically focusing on its debt levels and balance sheet. It concludes that Merck's debt is manageable, with net debt at 1.0 times EBITDA and strong interest coverage. Despite a slight decline in EBIT, the company's free cash flow generation suggests it can comfortably handle its current debt.

title: Applied Therapeutics Appoints John H. Johnson as Executive Chairman
summary: Applied Therapeutics, Inc. announced significant leadership changes, appointing John H. Johnson as Executive Chairman and Les Funtleyder as Interim Chief Executive Officer. Dr. Shoshana Shendelman has stepped down as Chair and CEO. The company also provided updates on its govorestat drug for Classic Galactosemia and Sorbitol Dehydrogenase (SORD) Deficiency, including receiving a Complete Response Letter from the FDA and withdrawing its European MAA for govorestat for Classic Galactosemia.

title: Regeneron says study data support big bet on new blood thinners
summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals plans to advance two Factor XI-blocking blood thinners, REGN7508 and REGN9933, into Phase 3 clinical trials in 2025. This decision follows positive mid-stage study results where the drugs, which aim to be safer alternatives to existing anticoagulants, matched or outperformed marketed medicines in preventing blood clots after knee replacement surgery. The company is seeking to bolster its pipeline amid challenges to its leading eye drug Eylea and recent FDA rejections for two blood cancer drugs.

title: The RESTORE Patent Rights Act: One Little Sentence that Could Change Everything
summary: The RESTORE Patent Rights Act, a one-sentence bill, proposes to create a rebuttable presumption for permanent injunctions in patent infringement cases, partially reversing the 2006 eBay Inc. v. MercExchange ruling. A Senate Judiciary Committee hearing revealed deep divisions, with supporters arguing it prevents "efficient infringement" and critics warning it could benefit "patent trolls" and stifle innovation. The debate centers on balancing patent holders' exclusive rights with broader economic and social impacts, questioning whether the U.S. patent system should return to pre-eBay practices or adopt more flexible international models.

title: Senior Living Market to Grow by USD 127.37 Billion (2024-2028), Aging Baby Boomer Population Driving Growth, Market Evolution Powered by AI - Technavio
summary: The global senior living market is projected to grow by USD 127.37 billion from 2024-2028, driven primarily by the aging baby boomer population and increasing technological advancements in long-term healthcare, particularly AI-powered solutions. While the market anticipates a CAGR of 5.9%, it faces challenges such as staffing shortages, high turnover rates, and rising operational costs coupled with regulatory burdens. North America is expected to contribute 40% to this market growth, with assisted living and independent living segments showing strong expansion.

title: Marketing Mix of Bausch and Lomb and 4Ps (Updated 2026)
summary: This article analyzes the marketing mix of Bausch and Lomb, focusing on its Product, Price, Place, and Promotion strategies. It details the company's extensive range of eye health products, its global distribution network, strategic pricing models, and sophisticated promotional approaches. The piece highlights Bausch and Lomb's commitment to innovation and quality, underscoring its long-standing reputation in the healthcare segment.

title: A shift toward RLE
summary: Refractive Lens Exchange (RLE) is becoming a more popular refractive surgery choice due to advancements in IOL technology, offering solutions for presbyopic patients and those with hyperopia who are not ideal LASIK candidates. Experts Marjan Farid, D. Rex Hamilton, and Luke Rebenitsch discuss patient selection, advanced IOL options like TECNIS Odyssey and Light Adjustable Lens, and the importance of managing patient expectations, particularly regarding potential nighttime dysphotopsias and the need for enhancements. They also highlight the growth in RLE procedures, with some practices seeing it surpass other refractive surgery volumes.

title: ViiV Healthcare announces CHMP positive opinion for Vocabria + Rekambys, the first and only complete long-acting HIV treatment, for adolescents in Europe
summary: ViiV Healthcare, majority-owned by GSK, has received a positive opinion from the CHMP for Vocabria + Rekambys, a long-acting injectable HIV treatment, for adolescents aged 12 and older in Europe. This treatment could significantly reduce dosing from 365 to 6 times per year, addressing challenges like adherence and stigma for young people living with HIV. The positive opinion is supported by data from the MOCHA study, showing high rates of viral suppression and preference for the injectable regimen among adolescents.

title: AstraZeneca Says Andexxa Will Not Convert To Full Approval, Quietly Reveals Complete Response
summary: AstraZeneca's Andexxa, an accelerated approval product, will not be converting to full approval, a decision quietly revealed by the company. This news positions Andexxa as the latest drug to remain in limbo after failing to meet the requirements for full approval, highlighting ongoing challenges with the accelerated approval pathway.

title: Amgen to build $1B North Carolina pharmaceutical plant
summary: Amgen is investing $1 billion to construct a second drug substance manufacturing facility in Holly Springs, North Carolina, building on a previously announced $550 million plant in the same location. This expansion aims to increase capacity, meet global demand, and will create 370 new jobs, bringing Amgen's total investment in the city to over $1.5 billion. The company highlighted North Carolina's strong biomanufacturing ecosystem and skilled workforce as key factors in its decision, further solidifying the state's position as a hub for pharmaceutical manufacturing.

title: Johnson & Johnson awards its US and Canada media business to WPP
summary: Johnson & Johnson has awarded its media business for the US and Canada to WPP. In 2023, J&J's worldwide advertising expenses, including TV, radio, print, and internet advertising, totaled $500 million. The specific agencies under WPP that will handle the account were not detailed in this report.

title: ASH 2024: Arcellx publishes positive results for Anito-cel in multiple myeloma
summary: Arcellx presented positive Phase II results for its cell therapy, Anito-cel, for relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma at the ASH 2024 meeting. Anito-cel showed a 100% overall response rate and a 76% stringent complete response rate, comparable to established therapies like Johnson & Johnson's Carvykti, but with a potentially more favorable safety profile. Despite strong clinical data and a partnership with Gilead, Anito-cel faces an uphill battle against existing market leaders given their broader label expansions and projected higher sales.

title: How Trump’s second term will affect the medtech industry
summary: Donald Trump's potential second term could bring nuanced changes to the medtech industry, with key areas to watch including a more favorable environment for M&A, the impact of potential tariffs on critical supply chains, and unconventional leadership at federal health agencies. While some analysts believe medtech is relatively well-positioned, companies will need to adapt to shifts in regulatory enforcement and international trade policies. The future of the FDA's LDT rule also remains uncertain, dependent on ongoing legal challenges and the new administration's stance.

title: Vicarious Surgical CFO William Kelly stepping down in January
summary: Vicarious Surgical's CFO William Kelly is resigning in January 2025 to pursue other career opportunities, leading the company to begin a succession process. While analyst Ryan Zimmerman noted that Kelly's departure doesn't "instill confidence," he believes it will not impact the surgical robot developer's timeline to market. Vicarious Surgical is developing a single-port surgical robot and is expected to apply for FDA clearance in early to mid-2026.

title: Pharmaceutical Packaging Market Is Expected To Reach Revenue Of USD 583.5 Bn By 2033, At 16.0% CAGR: Dimension Market Research
summary: The global pharmaceutical packaging market is projected to reach USD 583.5 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 16.0% from USD 153.7 billion in 2024. This growth is driven by the increasing need for advanced, sustainable, and specialized packaging for various drug types, especially biologics and personalized medicine. North America is expected to hold the largest market share, with primary packaging and plastic/polymer materials dominating their respective segments.

title: 8 New 4-Star Stocks
summary: This article identifies eight new 4-star rated stocks and three new 5-star rated stocks according to Morningstar's valuation metrics for the week ended December 6. Companies like Johnson & Johnson and General Dynamics are highlighted as newly undervalued, with detailed metrics on their returns, fair value estimates, and uncertainty ratings. The broader market context shows that while the US market is moderately overvalued, a significant portion of Morningstar-covered stocks are still considered undervalued.

title: Coca-Cola vs. Procter & Gamble: Which Is the Better Dow Dividend King Stock to Buy Before the End of the Year?
summary: This article compares Coca-Cola and Procter & Gamble, both Dow Dividend Kings, to determine which is a better investment by the end of the year. While both face challenges with sales volume, Coca-Cola is highlighted for its better valuation and higher dividend yield. Procter & Gamble, despite its strong capital return program and operating margins, trades above its historical P/E average, whereas Coke is below.

title: Amgen plans $1B investment in North Carolina
summary: Amgen, Inc. announced a $1 billion investment in a manufacturing expansion in Holly Springs, North Carolina, which is expected to create 370 jobs. The biopharmaceutical company will receive substantial state and local incentives, including a Job Development Investment grant of up to $4.8 million and potential local incentives of up to $40 million. This investment highlights North Carolina's growing prominence as a biotechnology hub, attracting major companies due to its skilled workforce and business climate.

title: Assessing Functional Similarity of Biosimilar ABP 654 and Ustekinumab in Samples from Patients with Crohn’s Disease
summary: This study assessed the functional similarity of ABP 654, an FDA-approved interchangeable biosimilar, to ustekinumab reference product (RP) by evaluating their inhibition of IL-23 and IL-12 pathways in peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) from healthy donors and Crohn's disease patients. The research, using various in vitro pharmacology studies, found that ABP 654 demonstrated similar inhibition of key inflammatory markers like IFN-γ, STAT3/STAT4 phosphorylation, and IL-17 release as ustekinumab RP. These findings contribute to the totality of evidence supporting the biosimilarity and interchangeability of ABP 654.

title: Johnson & Johnson Is A Better Dividend Stock Than Amgen In Our View (NYSE:JNJ)
summary: This article argues that Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) is a superior dividend stock compared to Amgen (AMGN), especially in uncertain market conditions. Citing Peter Lynch's insights, the author rates JNJ as a strong buy due to its yield, dividend consistency, valuation, and inventory management, while downgrading AMGN to a hold. The analysis is based on JNJ's robust profile across several key financial metrics.

title: Global Antibiotics Market is Expected to Reach ~USD 59 Billion by 2030 | DelveInsight
summary: The global antibiotics market is projected to reach approximately USD 59 billion by 2030, driven by the increasing prevalence of infectious diseases, higher consumption in developing countries, intensified R&D efforts, and a growing geriatric population. Asia-Pacific is expected to dominate this growth due to a high incidence of infectious diseases and the presence of numerous regional manufacturers. Recent approvals of Tedizolid Phosphate Tablets by CSPC Pharmaceutical Group and ORLYNVAH by FDA highlight ongoing innovation in the sector.

title: Benzos to Breakthroughs: Bill Burns on Roche’s Transformation
summary: This article features an interview with Bill Burns about the transformation of Roche. It is part of a weekly bulletin from Scrip, covering top pharma business news. The article highlights Roche's evolution from its early focus to current breakthroughs, as discussed by Bill Burns.

title: Even at 10% Discount, Eylea Biosimilar Pavblu Offers Lower-Cost Option
summary: Amgen Inc. has launched its Eylea biosimilar, Pavblu, at a 10% discount, despite ongoing patent infringement lawsuits with Regeneron. The "at-risk" launch positions Pavblu as a lower-cost option in a competitive market for ocular VEGF inhibitors, though its impact on market share remains uncertain due to existing cheaper alternatives and prescribers' particular preferences. Experts note the financial risks for Amgen but also the potential advantages of being first to market, especially with the looming Inflation Reduction Act's impact on drug pricing.

title: We have made strategic choices for Indian market to grow, scale and meet unmet needs of consumers: Manish Anandani, MD, Kenvue India
summary: Manish Anandani, MD of Kenvue India, discusses the company's strategic growth plans for the Indian consumer health market since its spin-off from Johnson & Johnson in 2023. Kenvue is focusing on premiumization, enhancing consumer engagement, and expanding its portfolio in key segments like Self-Care, Essential Health, and Skin Health to address unmet consumer needs and capitalize on India's significant growth potential. The company emphasizes science-backed, doctor-recommended products and increasing health literacy among consumers.

title: Inside Johnson & Johnson's JLABS Incubator in Torrey Pines San Diego
summary: Johnson & Johnson's JLABS in Torrey Pines, San Diego, is a prominent incubator nurturing biotech startups in four strategic areas: consumer health, pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and digital health. Led by Nick Mourlas, JLABS provides capital-efficient lab space, mentorship, and extensive networking opportunities, helping companies like Poseida Therapeutics and Rapport Therapeutics achieve significant milestones. The incubator also leverages San Diego's rich ecosystem of research institutions and talent to foster innovation.

title: J&J appoints Shawn Millerick to lead vision care unit in the Americas
summary: Johnson & Johnson has appointed Shawn Millerick as the new president of its vision care unit for North and Latin America. Millerick, who has previous experience at Abbott and Alcon, will be responsible for defining strategy and driving growth for the business, which includes Acuvue contact lenses. This appointment follows a recovery in J&J's vision business, which saw 3.5% sales growth in the third quarter after earlier declines, primarily driven by contact lens price adjustments.

title: Why Is Moderna, Inc. (MRNA) Among the Best High Growth Healthcare Stocks to Invest In Now?
summary: This article examines why Moderna, Inc. (MRNA) is considered a high-growth healthcare stock, highlighting its strong revenue growth and advancements in mRNA technology, particularly in cancer vaccines and its successful COVID-19 vaccine, Spikevax. It also touches on broader trends in global healthcare spending, emphasizing the defensive nature of healthcare investments and the impact of GLP-1 agonists in pharmaceuticals.

title: FDA approves Stanford Medicine-developed drug that treats rare heart disease
summary: The FDA has approved acoramidis (Attruby), a drug developed at Stanford Medicine through its SPARK program, for treating transthyretin amyloid cardiomyopathy (ATTR-CM), a rare heart disease. The drug, originally created by Isabella Graef and Mamoun Alhamadsheh, prevents the misfolding of a protein that causes heart stiffness. Clinical trials showed improved efficacy, survival rates, and quality of life for patients compared to existing treatments.

title: Roche to acquire Poseida Therapeutics for $1.5bn
summary: Roche has announced its intent to acquire Poseida Therapeutics in a deal valued at $1.5 billion, which includes Poseida's CAR-T therapy assets and manufacturing capabilities. This acquisition aims to accelerate Roche's development in allogeneic cell therapy, building on an existing partnership. The transaction is expected to close in the first quarter of 2025, pending customary closing conditions.

title: Halozyme withdraws $2B bid for Evotec after German biotech was 'unwilling to engage'
summary: Halozyme Therapeutics has withdrawn its $2.1 billion offer to acquire Evotec after the German biotech company refused to engage in discussions. Halozyme CEO Helen Torley stated that Evotec's leadership was unwilling to explore a potential combination, citing the company's public commitment to remaining independent. Evotec's share price plummeted after the news, despite its leadership expressing "strong conviction" in its standalone strategy.

title: Autolus Latest Competitor in Global CAR-T Frenzy with FDA Approval of Aucatzyl
summary: The U.S. FDA has approved Autolus Inc.'s Aucatzyl (obecabtagene autoleucel), marking the seventh CAR-T cell therapy in the U.S. and the 11th globally for adults with relapsed or refractory B-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia. This approval introduces a new competitor in the CAR-T market, particularly against Gilead’s Tecartus, offering a personalized treatment potentially with fewer side effects. The efficacy of Aucatzyl was demonstrated in the FELIX trial, showing a 42% complete remission rate within three months.

title: Medtronic acquires Fortimedix Surgical to boost surgical portfolio
summary: Medtronic has acquired Netherlands-based Fortimedix Surgical to enhance its surgical and endoscopy portfolio, particularly in articulating instruments for minimally invasive and robotic surgeries. This acquisition comes as Medtronic's surgical and endoscopy sales experienced a slight decrease in the last quarter, while the endoscopy device market is projected for significant growth. Fortimedix Surgical's technology is expected to help Medtronic capitalize on the increasing adoption of advanced surgical techniques.

title: After ProfoundBio buy, Genmab open to more M&A if it makes 'strategic sense,’ CFO says
summary: Genmab, celebrating its 25th anniversary, is shifting from a partner-based model to an asset owner-focused model, driven by its CFO Anthony Pagano. Following its first major acquisition of ProfoundBio, Genmab is open to further M&A opportunities that align strategically, particularly those with similar characteristics to Rina-S in terms of modality and therapeutic area. The company is also exploring expansion into immunology in addition to its core oncology focus, leveraging its ADC and C3-based T-cell engaging bispecific antibody programs.

title: J&J, Merck Cut Jobs in China as Local Competition Takes Toll
summary: Johnson & Johnson and Merck & Co. are reducing their workforces in China due to increasing competition from domestic pharmaceutical companies. These job cuts affect J&J's general surgery division and Merck's diabetes unit, as Beijing's campaign to lower medical costs pressures profit margins for foreign drug giants.

title: Labcorp hires Julia Wang as CFO
summary: Labcorp has announced the appointment of Julia Wang as its new Executive Vice President and CFO, effective December 2. She will succeed Glenn Eisenberg, who is retiring after 10 years but will remain as a special adviser to the CEO through April 2025 to assist with the transition. Wang brings extensive strategic and financial experience from previous roles at Beigene, Alexion Pharmaceuticals, Quest Diagnostics, and Johnson & Johnson.

title: Merck says Keytruda injection on par with approved IV version in trial
summary: Merck announced that a late-stage trial showed its injectable version of the cancer drug Keytruda was non-inferior to the current intravenous formulation, improving accessibility and ease of administration. This development could help protect Keytruda, which had $25 billion in sales last year, from competition when the IV version loses exclusivity. The injectable form takes 2-3 minutes to administer compared to 30 minutes for the IV drip and demonstrated comparable efficacy in patient exposure and concentration.

title: RFK Jr. may start a food and drug ‘culture war.’ Investors are running for cover.
summary: Donald Trump's nomination of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to lead the Department of Health and Human Services has raised concerns among investors due to his vows to reform the food and drug industries. This move is seen as one of several unorthodox appointments by President-elect Trump. The HHS secretary position oversees a massive budget of over $1.7 trillion for healthcare products and services, causing investors to scramble to understand the potential market impact.

title: J&J pill clears skin in two late-stage psoriasis studies
summary: Johnson & Johnson (J&J) announced positive Phase 3 trial results for its experimental oral immune disease drug, icotrokinra, developed with Protagonist Therapeutics. The drug significantly cleared skin in most participants with moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis, showing efficacy comparable to older injectable drugs like Stelara and potentially superior to other oral psoriasis treatments. While less effective than Skyrizi and Tremfya, J&J anticipates icotrokinra could achieve over $5 billion in peak annual sales by expanding the IL-23 drug market.

title: Simplify Health Care ETF Holdings
summary: The Simplify Health Care ETF (PINK) was established on October 7, 2021, and focuses on health care equity investments, currently managing $237.79 million in assets across 53 holdings. It is an actively managed fund that donates its net profit from annual management fees to the Susan G. Komen Breast Cancer Foundation. The article lists its latest holdings, including top positions in UNITED THERAPEUTICS CORP, PURECYCLE TECHNOLOGIES, and NOVO-NORDISK A S, and details recent changes in its portfolio, especially frequent adjustments involving AstraZeneca and cash.

title: Eli Lilly follows J&J in suing Biden administration over 340B rebates
summary: Eli Lilly is suing the federal government over its plans to offer rebates instead of upfront discounts for drugs under the 340B program, following a similar lawsuit by Johnson & Johnson. Drugmakers argue that these rebates are necessary to ensure program integrity and prevent abuses, while regulators maintain the current system requires upfront discounts. Both companies are challenging HRSA's guidance, seeking court rulings to allow their rebate models.

title: Eli Lilly, J&J Sue US Over Rejected Changes To Drug Discounting Program
summary: Eli Lilly and Johnson & Johnson have filed lawsuits against the U.S. government after their proposed changes to the decades-old 340B drug discounting program were rejected. The companies argue that the program, which requires them to sell discounted medications to certain medical facilities, has flaws allowing some hospitals to profit unfairly, and their proposed "rebate" model would better serve its original intent while increasing transparency. The Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA), which oversees the program, has rejected these changes and warned of fines for non-compliance.

title: NRG Energy Honors Business Customers During Fifth Annual Excellence in Energy Awards
summary: NRG Energy honored business customers at its fifth annual Excellence in Energy Awards, recognizing those who demonstrated leadership in sustainability, community support, and efficient energy management. Awards were presented virtually across various categories including resilience, sustainability, and community engagement, highlighting innovative energy solutions and positive impacts. The event underscored the company's commitment to partnering with businesses to achieve energy goals and make a positive difference.

title: 4 Ways to Lock In Yields Above 5%
summary: With the Federal Reserve likely to begin cutting interest rates, investors are looking for ways to lock in current high yields. Strategies like multiyear guaranteed annuities and defined-maturity ETFs can help preserve income for one to five years. This proactive approach aims to counter the expected erosion of yields in money-market funds and high-yield savings accounts once rates decline.

title: Japan Joins the Rate-Hike Club As Rate Cuts Loom
summary: The Bank of Japan raised interest rates for the first time in 17 years, ending an eight-year experiment with negative rates. This move led to a sell-off in the yen and a rally in the Nikkei 225, contradicting typical market reactions to rate hikes but mirroring prior responses to negative rate implementations. The article suggests that negative rates act as a tax on the banking system, and their cessation could paradoxically stimulate economic growth, a trend observed in experiments with negative rates in Europe as well.

title: KeyCorp Deal Could Be a Model for Regional Banks
summary: KeyCorp's recent sale of a nearly 15% stake to Canada’s Bank of Nova Scotia for $2.8 billion has been met with a positive market reaction, with KeyCorp shares surging over 9%. This deal is seen as a potential model for other regional banks to build capital and reposition their bond portfolios. The market's upbeat response to this strategic move suggests it could be a blueprint for regional lenders navigating current economic landscapes.

title: Bob Hoyt
summary: Bob Hoyt was appointed Group Chief Legal Officer of HSBC Holdings plc in January 2021. He previously served as Group General Counsel at Barclays plc and General Counsel and Chief Regulatory Affairs Officer for PNC Financial Services Group. Hoyt also held positions at the US Department of the Treasury and as Special Assistant and Associate Counsel to US President George W. Bush.

title: A major finance firm is requiring its 35,000 employees to take a generative AI bootcamp
summary: S&P Global is partnering with Accenture to put all 35,000 of its employees through a "comprehensive gen AI learning program" starting this month. This initiative aims to equip S&P Global's workforce with AI fluency, addressing the financial service industry's increasing reliance on AI and AI talent. The collaboration also extends to AI development and benchmarking across the financial services industry, combining Accenture's Foundation Model Services with S&P AI Benchmarks by Kensho.

title: YPF in $500 Million Offering and Concurrent Tender and Exchange Offers
summary: Cleary Gottlieb represented YPF S.A. in a successful $500 million offering of its 8.75% senior notes due 2031, which priced on September 4, 2024. Concurrently, YPF launched tender and exchange offers for existing senior notes, aiming to purchase or exchange up to $500 million in outstanding debt. These concurrent offers are contingent on the successful closure of the new bond offering, reinforcing YPF's position as Argentina's leading energy company.

title: Corporate Bonds Risks, Returns Vs. Equities, Treasuries
summary: This article examines the risk and return profiles of corporate bonds relative to sovereign bonds (U.S. Treasuries) and equities. It highlights how investment-grade corporate bonds behave similarly to Treasuries with some equity correlation, while high-yield bonds strongly correlate with equities. The article also discusses how corporate bond and equity markets can diverge during different economic cycles, especially in the late stages of expansions, and points out current downside risks for corporate bonds due to rising consumer loan defaults.

title: Carlyle to buy Advance Auto Parts unit
summary: Carlyle Group Inc. is acquiring Advance Auto Parts Inc.’s Worldpac unit for $1.5 billion, marking its first major industrial investment in over two years. This divestiture comes as Advance Auto Parts faces pressure from shareholder activists to improve compensation and compete with rivals like AutoZone. The deal follows Advance Auto Parts' announcement to cut its net sales forecast for the year, causing its stock to drop.

title: Sally Beauty Holdings Announces the Completion of a Registered Public Offering of $600 Million of Senior Notes Due 2032
summary: Sally Beauty Holdings, through its subsidiaries Sally Holdings LLC and Sally Capital Inc., announced the completion of a registered public offering of $600 million aggregate principal amount of Senior Notes due 2032. The proceeds from this offering, along with existing credit facilities and cash, will be used to redeem all outstanding $679.96 million aggregate principal amount of senior unsecured notes due 2025. The redemption is expected to be completed on or about March 13, 2024, with BofA Securities, J.P. Morgan, and Truist Securities acting as joint book-running managers.

title: Wells Fargo has fired a bunch of employees after finding out they were pretending to work
summary: Wells Fargo has fired over a dozen employees from its wealth and investment management team for "simulating keyboard activity" to appear busy while not actually working. This incident, revealed in a FINRA filing, highlights the use of "mouse jigglers" and similar tools by remote workers seeking to avoid detection. The bank's action supports the wider Wall Street push for employees to return to the office, contrasting with the benefits experts have noted for hybrid work models.

title: Li Lu – Top 10 Holdings – Latest 13F
summary: This article details the top 10 holdings of superinvestor Li Lu's portfolio as of June 30, 2024, based on his latest 13F-HR filing. His portfolio has a market value of over $2.5 billion, with significant concentrations in companies like Bank of America, Alphabet, Berkshire Hathaway, and Apple. The article emphasizes using 13F-HR documents to track superinvestors' strategies.

title: Invitation Homes Announces Pricing of $500 Million of 4.875% Senior Notes due 2035
summary: Invitation Homes Inc. announced the pricing of a $500 million aggregate principal amount of 4.875% Senior Notes due 2035 by its operating partnership. The notes were priced at 98.855% of the principal amount and are expected to close on September 26, 2024, subject to customary closing conditions. The net proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including debt repayment, working capital, acquisitions, and renovations of single-family properties.

title: Pennymac Announces Organizational Changes
summary: PennyMac Financial Services, Inc. and PennyMac Mortgage Investment Trust announced that William Chang is stepping down from his roles as Senior Managing Director and Chief Capital Markets Officer for PFSI and Chief Investment Officer for PMT. Mark Elbaum, an industry veteran who joined Pennymac last year, will assume these responsibilities on an interim basis. Chairman and CEO David Spector expressed confidence in Elbaum's ability to lead the capital markets group and thanked Chang for his decade of service.

title: Paysafe Partners with Deutsche Bank to Strengthen Its Cash Services for Postbank Customers in Germany
summary: Paysafe has partnered with Deutsche Bank to enhance cash services for its customers in Germany, starting with Postbank in November and expanding to Deutsche Bank in H2 2025. This collaboration utilizes Paysafe's barcode solution, Paysafecash, allowing customers to easily deposit and withdraw cash at 12,500 retail locations without needing to visit a bank branch or ATM. This initiative aims to digitalize cash services and underscores the continued importance of cash in a digital world.

title: Warren Buffett’s BofA stake is shrinking so much, it’s near a key threshold for regulators
summary: Berkshire Hathaway's stake in Bank of America has decreased to 11.4%, nearing a regulatory threshold where sales disclosures would become quarterly instead of within a few days. Warren Buffett's selling spree, which began in mid-July without a stated reason, has generated $6.2 billion for Berkshire. The most recent sales involved 21 million shares sold for $848 million between August 28 and 30.

title: Premier completes strategic review process, authorizes $1B buyback
summary: Premier's Board of Directors has concluded its strategic review, authorizing a new $1.0 billion share repurchase program. This follows the divestiture of its non-healthcare GPO operations in July 2023. The company has already entered into an accelerated share repurchase agreement for $400 million with Bank of America.

title: WaFd Bank Completes Sale of $2.8 Billion in Multi-Family Loans
summary: WaFd Bank announced the sale of approximately $2.8 billion in multi-family commercial real estate loans to Bank of America, which will then sell them to PIMCO. This sale, executed at no loss to WaFd and at 92% of the principal balance, provides immediate liquidity for debt reduction, loan origination, or stock buybacks. WaFd Bank emphasizes that the discount reflects changes in interest rates rather than the quality of these CRE loans, which originated from the Luther Burbank Savings acquisition.

title: Schwab only platforms third-party spot Bitcoin ETFs, mostly Fidelity and BlackRock, but don't be surprised if it 'shocks the world' with deep-cheap ETF, analyst says
summary: Schwab is currently offering third-party spot Bitcoin ETFs, primarily from Fidelity and BlackRock. An analyst suggests that Schwab could potentially introduce its own "deep-cheap" Bitcoin ETF, which would be a significant development in the market. This move could surprise the industry given Schwab's current platforming strategy.

title: KKR Used Equity Rather Than Debt to Fund Acquisition of Broker April Group
summary: KKR & Co. funded its €2.3 billion acquisition of French insurance broker April Group solely with equity, a departure from traditional debt-funded buyouts. This move reflects the current market environment where banks are reluctant to extend large loans due to existing buyout debt and rising interest rates. KKR plans to introduce debt financing later when terms are more favorable, highlighting a shift in leveraged finance as private credit funds partially fill the void left by banks.

title: 2024 Top Mortgage Professionals In Orlando
summary: Orlando Magazine has published its list of 2024 Top Mortgage Professionals in Orlando, compiled by DataJoe Research. The list includes a diverse group of preferred lenders and mortgage professionals selected through an online peer-voting process and research campaign. The article features their names, companies, and websites as a resource for future homebuyers.

title: Germany’s Deutsche Bank looks across the pond as it aims to become Wall Street’s most powerful European rival
summary: Deutsche Bank is ambitiously expanding its U.S. wealth management division, hiring senior private bankers in key cities like San Francisco and Los Angeles, to become a leading European rival to Wall Street firms. The bank aims for double-digit revenue growth despite stagnating wealth management revenues in Q1, focusing on ultra-high-net-worth individuals and leveraging its turnaround success since 2019. This strategic move aligns with its broader goal of growing wealth management in regions like Southeast Asia and the Middle East, while also benefiting from recent banking crises that have driven clients to consider foreign lenders.

title: M&T Bank's Profits Climb Thanks To Fee Growth And Loan Optimism
summary: M&T Bank reported a significant boost in third-quarter profits, driven by increased fees from institutional services and wealth management, and a strategic reduction in credit loss provisions. Despite a drop in net interest income, the bank maintained stability, leading to an increase in net income available to shareholders. M&T Bank's shares have surged, outperforming the S&P 500, indicating investor confidence in regional banks' resilience amidst Federal Reserve rate cuts.

title: T-Mobile Agrees to Sell $2.5 Billion of Senior Notes
summary: T-Mobile US, Inc. announced that its subsidiary, T-Mobile USA, Inc., has agreed to sell $2.5 billion in aggregate principal amount of senior notes through a registered public offering. The offering includes three tranches of notes due in 2029, 2035, and 2055, with closing expected on September 26, 2024. The net proceeds are intended for general corporate purposes, including share repurchases, dividends, and refinancing existing debt.

title: US regional banks dramatically step up loans to oil and gas
summary: US regional banks, including Citizens Financial Group, BOK Financial Corp., and Truist Securities Inc., are significantly increasing their lending to oil, gas, and coal companies. This shift is occurring as larger European rivals withdraw from such financing due to stricter climate regulations and ESG concerns. The move by regional US banks presents new opportunities for fossil-fuel borrowers and challenges assumptions about divestment policies.

title: Citi and Bank of America Say They’re Leaving Global Climate-Banking Alliance
summary: Citigroup Inc. and Bank of America Corp. announced their withdrawal from the Net-Zero Banking Alliance (NZBA), making them the latest Wall Street lenders to exit the global climate-banking group. Both banks stated their continued commitment to achieving net zero emissions and working with clients to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, despite leaving the alliance. Goldman Sachs and Wells Fargo had previously left the NZBA.

title: Honda-Nissan deal sets up next fight for bankers in Japan M&A boom
summary: Japan is experiencing record-breaking deal-making activity this year, with transaction volumes up 48% to roughly $200 billion, largely driven by local financial firms. The talks between Honda and Nissan for a potential acquisition have recently emerged as the latest high-profile deal, attracting significant attention from investment bankers. Nomura Holdings is advising Honda, while Mizuho Financial Group and Bank of America are advising Nissan.

title: Bank of America to Buy $990 Million of Multifamily Loans From HomeStreet
summary: Bank of America (BAC) has agreed to acquire approximately $990 million in multifamily commercial real estate loans from HomeStreet Inc. This transaction is expected to help HomeStreet return to profitability by allowing it to pay down higher-interest Federal Home Loan Bank advances and brokered deposits. The purchase price for the loans is approximately 92% of their unpaid principal balance.

title: JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, and Wells Fargo sued over Zelle scams that cost $870 million
summary: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) is suing JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, and Early Warning Services (which operates Zelle) for allegedly failing to protect users from fraud. The CFPB claims that customers have lost over $870 million due to inadequate safeguards on the Zelle network. The banks and Early Warning Services dispute the allegations, stating the lawsuit is flawed and that Zelle implements strong fraud prevention measures, with bank representatives calling the action a threat to the service.

title: Vesta Announces Closing of US$545 Million Global Syndicated Sustainable Credit Facility Including US$200 Million Revolving Credit Facility
summary: Corporación Inmobiliaria Vesta has closed a US$545 million Global Syndicated Sustainable Credit Facility, which includes a US$345 million term loan and a US$200 million Revolving Credit Facility. This financing provides Vesta with strategic liquidity reserves at a competitive cost to support its Route 2030 growth plan and prudent financial management. The credit facility also incorporates a sustainability pricing adjustment tied to Vesta's compliance with its annual KPI target for certified gross leasable area.

title: Rising Inflation May Challenge Stocks
summary: Morgan Stanley's Global Investment Committee believes the U.S. is entering a "reflationary" era where economic growth is accompanied by rising inflation, posing risks to equity markets. Recent data, including increasing CPI and PPI, and surging small business confidence, contradict the market's expectation for further Fed rate cuts and continued disinflation. Investors are advised to diversify portfolios away from concentrated tech stocks and consider sectors like financials, energy, and real estate, while expecting potential policy mistakes and a "stagflation" scenario in 2025.

title: Afectaría Trump a BBVA y Santander
summary: S&P Global warns that a second Trump presidency could significantly impact BBVA and Santander's growth in key markets like Mexico and the U.S. Proposed tariffs on Mexican auto imports and the abolition of electric vehicle tax credits would negatively affect these banks, with BBVA being Mexico's largest lender and Santander having benefited from EV credit schemes. Analysts suggest investors would view a Trump presidency unfavorably for these financial institutions, potentially leading to slower Mexican economic growth and weaker bank lending.

title: Benchstrength Closes $62M Early-Stage Venture Capital Fund
summary: Benchstrength, a NYC-based venture capital firm founded by former General Catalyst investors Ken Chenault Jr. and John Monagle, has announced the close of its first $62 million early-stage fund. The firm focuses on investing in pre-seed and seed stage technology companies across various sectors, particularly those with historically overlooked founders. The fund's investors include prominent institutions like the Ford Foundation, GCM Grosvenor, and Goldman Sachs Asset Management, as well as notable individual investors.

title: BofA’s Moynihan Says Regulators Need ‘Sober Look’ at Bank Rules
summary: Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan stated that regulators should reassess and relax bank rules, potentially leading to significant industry changes under a Trump administration. Moynihan emphasized the need for a "sober look at the right regulation" for the banking sector. He also anticipates rate cuts and election enthusiasm will boost consumer spending.

title: No bull: Merrill relocating its statue from Deerwood Park North to the Bank of America campus
summary: Bank of America is relocating the Merrill Lynch bull statue from its Deerwood Park North campus to its Gramercy Woods location, signaling that the former Merrill Lynch property could become available for other use. This move sparks speculation that Intercontinental Exchange Inc. (ICE), which is establishing its ICE Mortgage Technology division headquarters in Jacksonville, might be interested in the Deerwood Park North site. ICE recently acquired Black Knight for $11.9 billion and is expanding its presence in Jacksonville, with the city offering $21 million in incentives for their expansion, which includes retaining 1,500 jobs and adding 500 more.

title: BofA Says Stocks Flash Sell Signal After Cash Holdings Tumble
summary: Bank of America strategists indicate a potential "sell" signal for global equities as fund managers have reduced cash holdings to a record low of 3.9% of assets under management. This historical pattern of declining cash allocation has previously preceded losses in the MSCI All-Country World Index. Concurrently, allocation to US equities has reached a record high, with fund managers being 36% overweight.

title: Spain Is Said to Hire Bank of America for CaixaBank Trading Plan
summary: Spain's bank resolution authority has reportedly hired Bank of America Corp. to manage a "dribble-out trading program" for its shares in CaixaBank SA. This initiative aims to offset the impact of share buybacks and maintain the government's stake in CaixaBank at approximately 18%, as stated by Economy Minister Carlos Cuerpo. The details of the trading plan are private.

title: Bank of America Declares Preferred Stock Dividends for First Quarter 2025
summary: Bank of America Corporation announced its Board of Directors has authorized regular cash dividends for various series of preferred stock for the first quarter of 2025. Dividends vary per series, with different record and payment dates in January and February 2025. These dividends cover series such as Convertible Preferred Stock, Series L, and several others with both fixed and fixed-to-floating rates.

title: Exclusive: Galicia Considers Stock Issuance and Calls for Mortgage Securitization
summary: Fabián Kon, CEO of Grupo Financiero Galicia, stated that Argentina needs a mortgage securitization market to drive credit growth, similar to models in the US. He also indicated that Galicia might consider a new stock issuance in 2026-2027 if Argentina’s economic rebound proves sustainable and credit demand continues to recover. Kon emphasized that mortgage securitization would free up bank lending capacity and inject life into the real estate market, while also noting the transformation of the Argentine financial system with banks reducing public bond exposure and increasing private sector lending.

title: Goldman Sachs Gets Regulatory Approval for Fund Sales in China
summary: Goldman Sachs has received regulatory approval from China to sell funds, a significant step for the U.S. investment bank's expansion in the country's financial market. This approval, following Morgan Stanley's recent clearance, signals China's ongoing commitment to opening its financial sector to foreign firms. Goldman Sachs is also refocusing its operations on core investment banking and trading, having scaled back its consumer banking footprint through various sales and divestitures.

title: Candel Therapeutics Closes $92 Million Public Offering to Advance Cancer Immunotherapy Development
summary: Candel Therapeutics successfully closed a public offering, raising approximately $92 million through the sale of common stock and pre-funded warrants. The funds will be primarily used to advance its cancer immunotherapy product candidates, particularly CAN-2409 for prostate cancer, and for general corporate purposes. The offering saw participation from major financial institutions, though it also implied potential share dilution and noted risks associated with clinical-stage development.

title: Anteris Technologies Global Corp. Announces Closing of $88.8 Million U.S. Initial Public Offering
summary: Anteris Technologies Global Corp. (ATGC) announced the closing of its $88.8 million U.S. initial public offering of 14,800,000 shares at $6.00 per share, with an additional 2,220,000 shares available via an underwriters' option. The common stock began trading on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker "AVR" on December 13, 2024, and its CHESS Depositary Interests will list on the ASX on December 17, 2024, also under "AVR." Proceeds are primarily earmarked for the development of its DurAVR® transcatheter heart valve and a global pivotal study.

title: A Look at Oakmark and MFS’ New Active ETFs
summary: Oakmark and MFS have launched new active ETFs, providing investors with versions of their established strategies. These ETFs share management teams and processes with their mutual fund counterparts but feature slight differences such as concentration and market cap focus. The new offerings aim to provide more tax-efficient access to active management, often at similar or lower fee levels than their mutual fund cousins.

title: Bitcoin ETF Flows in 2025 Will Exceed 2024: Bitwise
summary: Bitwise Asset Management predicts that Bitcoin ETF flows in 2025 will exceed those of 2024, ushering in a "golden age of crypto." The crypto fund manager's bullish outlook includes price targets of $200,000 for BTC, $7,000 for ETH, and $750 for SOL, driven by institutional investment and wirehouse approvals. Bitwise cites historical gold ETF flows and increasing investor allocations as reasons for sustained momentum in the crypto market.

title: Bank of America Promotes 387 to Managing Director, 16% More Than Last Year
summary: Bank of America (BAC) promoted 387 employees to Managing Director, a 16% increase from last year. Over half of those promoted were women or people of color, marking the fifth consecutive year that historically underrepresented groups constituted the majority of the class, reflecting BofA's commitment to diversity.

title: Google Parent Alphabet's Stock Soars to All-Time High
summary: Shares of Google parent Alphabet surged to an all-time high after the company unveiled its new AI model, Gemini 2.0, and President-elect Donald Trump tapped Andrew Ferguson to lead the Federal Trade Commission, raising hopes for a friendlier regulatory stance. The stock's gains were also boosted by the presentation of its new quantum computing chip, Willow, with analysts noting Alphabet's continued innovation. Bank of America analysts maintained a "buy" rating and a $210 price target for Alphabet.

title: Rising Inflation Unlikely To Derail December Fed Cut
summary: Despite three consecutive months of rising consumer prices, overall inflation matching consensus estimates might still enable the Federal Reserve to implement a 25 basis point interest rate cut in December. While this offers some relief for the broader economy, the mortgage market continues to face significant challenges, with rising housing costs and declining mortgage credit availability impacting homebuyers and investors. Economists predict a more gradual pace of rate cuts into 2025, with mortgage rates likely settling in the mid-to-low 6 percent range by year-end, although sustained economic data could slow this pace.

title: MetLIfe and General Atlantic launch $1bn life reinsurer
summary: MetLife and private equity firm General Atlantic have announced the formation of Chariot Reinsurance, a Bermuda-based $1 billion life reinsurer. Chariot Re is expected to launch in the first half of 2025 with MetLife as a strategic partner, initially reinsuring approximately $10 billion in liabilities. MetLife and General Atlantic will each own about 15% of the equity, with Chubb serving as an anchor third-party investor.

title: MetLife and General Atlantic Announce the Formation of Chariot Re
summary: MetLife and General Atlantic announced the formation of Chariot Reinsurance, Ltd. ("Chariot Re"), a Bermuda-based life and annuity reinsurance company expected to launch in the first half of 2025. This new entity will have an initial combined equity investment of over $1 billion, with MetLife and General Atlantic each owning approximately 15%, and will feature a strategic reinsurance partnership with MetLife, initially reinsuring a $10 billion block of liabilities. Chariot Re will leverage the asset management capabilities of both MetLife Investment Management and General Atlantic, and will be led by Cynthia Smith as CEO.

title: The Nuts and Bolts of Enhabit’s 2025 Hospice Growth Strategy
summary: Enhabit Inc. (NYSE: EHAB) is anticipating significant hospice growth in 2025, driven by investments in technology, workforce development, and a strategic case management model, following a recent legal victory. The company is leveraging AI to improve efficiency and reduce administrative burdens, allowing clinical teams more time with patients, and plans to focus on de novo development. Enhabit also aims to advocate for improved home health and hospice support to policymakers, emphasizing the value proposition of these services and the impact of funding cuts on access to care.

title: Dollar Tree, squeezed by weak spending, appoints interim CEO
summary: Dollar Tree Inc. has appointed Michael C. Creedon Jr. as its interim chief executive officer as the company struggles with weak consumer spending and competition. This appointment comes as former CEO Richard W. Dreiling stepped down, and the discounter continues to review strategic alternatives for its Family Dollar chain. Dollar Tree's stock has plunged about 53% this year, reflecting challenges in the discount retail segment despite a general expectation of strong performance during economic downturns.

title: Is The Trade Desk signaling the rebound of digital advertising?
summary: The Trade Desk (TTD) reported strong Q4 2023 earnings, exceeding lowered revenue guidance and raising Q1 2024 guidance, signaling a potential rebound in digital advertising. The company's AI-powered platform, Kokai, and its position in Connected TV (CTV) and shopper marketing are expected to drive future growth. Analysts have upgraded price targets, citing improved advertiser sentiment and a neutral to positive impact from Google's cookie deprecation.

title: Amazon warehouse workers say they struggle to afford food, rent
summary: A new study reveals that despite Amazon.com Inc. raising wages to $15 an hour five years ago, half of its warehouse workers surveyed still struggle to afford food or housing. The study, conducted by the University of Illinois Chicago’s Center for Urban Economic Development, found high rates of food and housing insecurity among Amazon employees, with many relying on government assistance. This research fuels ongoing criticism of Amazon's employee treatment, particularly regarding working conditions and wage stagnation despite the company's growth.

title: Retailers Try on More Tech
summary: Retailers are entering a new phase of growth driven by AI and automation, according to Morgan Stanley. Key technologies like retail media, AI, and automation are expected to boost profitability and growth, particularly for larger retailers who can invest more in these advancements.
